The `ScopeExit` trigger and"] # [doc = " the `scope_id`/`sweep_epoch` are supplied at close time (from the region itself), not"] # [doc = " at deferral time — deferral is cheap (a single `ContentHash` push, no bookkeeping)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — the hash is stored as-is; no approximation."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct DeferredEntry { # [doc = " Content identity of the value deferred for reclamation (RFC-0027 §9 / RFC-0001 §4.6)."] pub value_meta_hash : ContentHash , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A scope-exit reclamation region (DN-32 §2.3 / RFC-0027 §10.3 / MEM-3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " One `Region` corresponds to one RT7 scope-tree node. It accumulates"] # [doc = " [`DeferredEntry`] values — values whose RC hit zero within this scope, or scope-local"] # [doc = " allocations — and at [`Region::close`] emits one [`ReclamationRecord`] per entry"] # [doc = " (trigger = `ScopeExit`) through a [`ReclamationSink`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ## Sweep order"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - **Parent–child TOTAL:** Close all child `Region`s before closing the parent."] # [doc = "   The child's epoch will be numerically lower than the parent's (it closed first),"] # [doc = "   encoding the child→root ordering as a number line. The caller (the executor) is"] # [doc = "   responsible for this invariant; [`ScopeTree::close_ordered`] enforces it in tests."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - **Siblings CONCURRENT (OQ-1 resolved, `Proven`-modulo-LR-9):** Sibling regions"] # [doc = "   close independently — no ordering constraint. Safe because LR-9 rules out"] # [doc = "   cross-sibling aliases (DN-32 §3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ## Never-silent contract (G2)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Every [`DeferredEntry`] pushed via [`Region::defer`] emits exactly one"] # [doc = " `ReclamationRecord(ScopeExit)` when [`Region::close`] is called. A `Region` dropped"] # [doc = " without calling `close` while still holding deferred entries is a G2 violation; in"] # [doc = " debug builds [`Region::drop`] panics to surface this."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — one record per deferred entry at close (enforced-by-construction)."] # [derive (Debug)] pub struct Region { # [doc = " The unique identity of this region in the scope tree."] pub id : ScopeNodeId , # [doc = " Deferred reclamation entries accumulated during this scope's lifetime."] deferred : Vec < DeferredEntry > , # [doc = " The epoch assigned at close time. `None` until `close()` is called."] closed_epoch : Option < RegionEpoch > , }
gaps.reason: struct `Region` uses named fields and references a type not resolvable in-file — emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: impl Region { # [doc = " Create a new, open region for a scope-tree node."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Allocates a fresh [`ScopeNodeId`]. The region starts open (no epoch yet)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — `ScopeNodeId` is unique and monotonically increasing."] # [must_use] pub fn new () -> Self { Region { id : ScopeNodeId :: allocate () , deferred : Vec :: new () , closed_epoch : None , } } # [doc = " Defer a value for scope-exit reclamation."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The value's content identity is pushed to the deferred list. At `close()`,"] # [doc = " a `ReclamationRecord(ScopeExit)` is emitted for it."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Calling `defer` after `close()` is a logic error; panics in debug builds."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — the entry is pushed exactly once; no approximation."] pub fn defer (& mut self , value_meta_hash : ContentHash) { debug_assert ! (self . closed_epoch . is_none () , "Region::defer called on a closed region (id={:?})" , self . id) ; self . deferred . push (DeferredEntry :: new (value_meta_hash)) ; } # [doc = " The number of values currently deferred in this region."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — returns the current length of the deferred list."] # [must_use] pub fn deferred_count (& self) -> usize { self . deferred . len () } # [doc = " Whether this region has been closed."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — `closed_epoch.is_some()` is the canonical indicator."] # [must_use] pub fn is_closed (& self) -> bool { self . closed_epoch . is_some () } # [doc = " The epoch assigned at close time, if this region has been closed."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns `None` if the region is still open."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — the epoch is the monotonic counter value at close time."] # [must_use] pub fn closed_epoch (& self) -> Option < RegionEpoch > { self . closed_epoch } # [doc = " Close this region: **batch-emit all deferred reclamations**, advance the monotonic"] # [doc = " epoch, and return the [`ClosedRegion`] summary."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ## What happens at close (`Exact` — enforced-by-construction)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " 1. A fresh [`RegionEpoch`] is allocated (monotonically increasing)."] # [doc = " 2. For each [`DeferredEntry`] in `self.deferred` (drained in order), exactly one"] # [doc = "    [`ReclamationRecord`] is constructed with:"] # [doc = "    - `scope_id = self.id.as_scope_id()`"] # [doc = "    - `sweep_epoch = epoch.as_sweep_epoch()`"] # [doc = "    - `trigger = ReclamationTrigger::ScopeExit`  ← **the SECOND live trigger (MEM-3)**"] # [doc = "    - `value_meta_hash = entry.value_meta_hash`"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = "    Each record is emitted through `sink.emit(record)` (never-silent G2)."] # [doc = " 3. The deferred list is drained (emptied)."] # [doc = " 4. `self.closed_epoch` is set to the allocated epoch."] # [doc = " 5. A [`ClosedRegion`] summary is returned."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ## Sweep-order contract (`Declared`)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The caller MUST close all child regions before calling `close()` on the parent."] # [doc = " A child closed before its parent will have a numerically lower `RegionEpoch`"] # [doc = " (it allocated its epoch first), encoding the child→root total order."] # [doc = " `Region::close` does NOT enforce this by type — it is an RT7 contract (live-executor"] # [doc = " wiring is downstream, FLAG)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ## Calling `close` more than once panics in debug builds."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Bulk-efficiency-as-perf-win: `Declared` (DN-32 §6a — no Mycelium measurement yet)."] pub fn close (& mut self , sink : & mut dyn ReclamationSink) -> ClosedRegion { debug_assert ! (self . closed_epoch . is_none () , "Region::close called on an already-closed region (id={:?})" , self . id) ; let epoch = RegionEpoch :: allocate () ; let scope_id = self . id . as_scope_id () ; let sweep_epoch = epoch . as_sweep_epoch () ; let count = self . deferred . len () ; for entry in self . deferred . drain (..) { let record = ReclamationRecord :: new (scope_id , sweep_epoch , ReclamationTrigger :: ScopeExit , entry . value_meta_hash ,) ; sink . emit (record) ; } self . closed_epoch = Some (epoch) ; ClosedRegion { id : self . id , epoch , reclaimed_count : count , } } }

impl Drop for Region { # [doc = " G2 guard: panic in debug builds if a region is dropped while still holding"] # [doc = " deferred entries without having been closed — that would silently lose audit records."] fn drop (& mut self) { # [cfg (debug_assertions)] if self . closed_epoch . is_none () && ! self . deferred . is_empty () { panic ! ("Region (id={:?}) dropped with {} deferred entries without calling close() — \
                 G2 violation: audit records would be silently lost" , self . id , self . deferred . len ()) ; } } }

Useful for supervision observability, property tests, and"] # [doc = " the sweep-order assertion (child epoch < parent epoch)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — all fields are deterministic functions of the close operation."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct ClosedRegion { # [doc = " The identity of the closed region."] pub id : ScopeNodeId , # [doc = " The monotonic epoch allocated at close time."] pub epoch : RegionEpoch , # [doc = " The number of deferred entries reclaimed (one `ScopeExit` record emitted per entry)."] pub reclaimed_count : usize , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A minimal parent–child scope-tree for encoding and testing the sweep-order model"] # [doc = " (RFC-0027 §10.3 / DN-32 §3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `ScopeTree` holds one parent [`Region`] and zero or more child [`Region`]s. The correct"] # [doc = " close order (RT7) is: close all children before the parent. [`ScopeTree::close_ordered`]"] # [doc = " enforces this order. Siblings may close in any order — they are concurrent (OQ-1)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is NOT a full runtime scope tree; it is the minimal structure needed to encode and"] # [doc = " property-test the parent–child TOTAL and sibling-CONCURRENT ordering guarantees. Live"] # [doc = " executor integration is downstream (FLAG)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Declared` — the tree structure encodes the sweep order; epoch-ordering"] # [doc = " property (child < parent) is `Exact` by monotonic counter."] pub struct ScopeTree { # [doc = " Child regions — independent siblings (closed before the parent, any order)."] pub children : Vec < Region > , # [doc = " The parent region — closed last."] pub parent : Region , }
gaps.reason: struct `ScopeTree` uses named fields and references a type not resolvable in-file — emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: impl ScopeTree { # [doc = " Create a `ScopeTree` with `n_children` child regions under one parent."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " All regions start open. Siblings are independent (no ordering constraint)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — `n_children + 1` unique `ScopeNodeId`s allocated."] # [must_use] pub fn new (n_children : usize) -> Self { ScopeTree { children : (0 .. n_children) . map (| _ | Region :: new ()) . collect () , parent : Region :: new () , } } # [doc = " Close all children first (in any order — siblings are concurrent), then close the parent."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns all [`ClosedRegion`] summaries in close order: children first, parent last."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The sweep-order invariant — child epoch < parent epoch — is guaranteed by the monotonic"] # [doc = " counter: children allocate their epochs before the parent does."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — close order is enforced; child epoch < parent epoch by construction."] pub fn close_ordered (& mut self , sink : & mut dyn ReclamationSink) -> Vec < ClosedRegion > { let mut results = Vec :: with_capacity (self . children . len () + 1) ; for child in & mut self . children { results . push (child . close (sink)) ; } results . push (self . parent . close (sink)) ; results } }
gaps.reason: no member of this impl block could be transpiled (2 sub-issue(s)): impl method `new` body: struct literal `ScopeTree { .. }` — not an in-file single-ctor struct or enum struct-variant that emits (no constructor to build; a cross-nodule variant is an honest DN-113/DN-134-OQ-2 resolvability gap, never a fabricated out-of-file reference — VR-5/G2); impl method `close_ordered` signature: unsupported Rust type form `dyn ReclamationSink`
